3 And David dwelt with -Achish at Gath, he and his men, every man with his household, even David with his two wives, Ahinoam the: Jezréelitess, and- Abigail: the Carmelitess, Nabal’s wife.
3 And David dwelt with -Achish at Gath, he and his men, every man with his household, even David with his two wives, Ahinoam the: Jezréelitess, and- Abigail: the Carmelitess, Nabal’s wife.
3 And David dwelt with -Achish at Gath, he and his men, every man with his household, even David with his two wives, Ahinoam the: Jezréelitess, and- Abigail: the Carmelitess, Nabal’s wife.

4. About this time David was become the father of six sons, born of as
many mothers. The eldest was by Ahinoam, and he was called Arenon; the
second was Daniel, by his wife Abigail; the name of the third was
Absalom, by Maacah, the daughter of Talmai, king of Geshur; the fourth
he named Adonijah, by his wife Haggith; the fifth was Shephatiah, by
Abital; the sixth he called Ithream, by Eglah. Now…

6. ous | and four years. Herod reigned for thirty-four years after Antigonus’s death, and thirty-seven after he had been declared king by the Romans.

they were sawn asunder Hezekiah's son, Manasseh, according to tradition, killed Isaiah by putting him in the trunk of a tree and sawing it in half.
